title: proc_arst: Unclear error message when (re)set polarity and sensitivity list do not agree
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Run `yosys -p "proc"` with the following file:
```verilog
module flop(input clk, global_reset, aload, aloaden, enable, data, output reg q);

wire reset, set;

assign reset = global_reset || (!aloaden && !aload);
assign set = !aloaden && aload;

always @(posedge clk, negedge reset, negedge set) begin
	if (reset) q <= 0;
	else if (set) q <= 1;
	else if (enable) q <= data;
end

endmodule
```

The bug here is that reset and set reset when high, but the sensitivity list triggers on their negative edge.

## Expected behavior

Perhaps something like:
```
ERROR: Async reset \reset resets when 1, but is listed as negative edge sensitive.
```

## Actual behavior

```
2.5. Executing PROC_ARST pass (detect async resets in processes).
Found async reset \reset in `\flop.$proc$flop.v:35$7'.
ERROR: Async reset \reset yields non-constant value 1'm for signal \q.
```
